title: Hope in a Hopeless World
speaker: Pastor Mohammad Yamout
source_files: Hope in a Hopeless World - GUEST SPEAKER_ Pastor Mohammad Yamout - 1 Timothy 4_6-11.txt

outline.title: Introduction: A Hopeless World and the Difference It Should Make
subpoints: The congregation just sang 'My Hope Is Built on Nothing Less' — the Spirit arranged the song to match the sermon; when you come to God's house, God has something for you
Pastor Yamout serves Tire Church in Tyre, Lebanon; Tini — supported by this congregation — ministers under his oversight

Hopelessness is evident at every level: COVID-19, civil wars in Syria/Yemen/Libya/Iraq/Afghanistan, Nagorno-Karabakh; Lebanon's collapse: corrupt leaders, economic crisis, Beirut explosion; America: despair over elections

The difference between believers and the world: 'for to this end we toil and strive because we have our hope set on the living God who is the Savior of all people, especially those who believe' (1 Timothy 4:10)

outline.title: Hope: The Third Pillar of Our Being
subpoints: 1 Corinthians 13:13: faith, hope, and love abide; if our hope is not built on a firm foundation, our faith and love will be wavering along with it
Hebrews 11:1: faith is the assurance of things hoped for; we can only have firm faith if we have an eternal hope
Our hope must be solid and strong; it cannot be built on things that do not last — on governments, bank accounts, health, family — 'I hope so' is no hope at all
Our hope must be built on the living God — the Alpha and Omega — and on the Savior, Jesus Christ, whose redemptive plan extends from the beginning of time to the end of time

outline.title: Why Our Hope Is Built on the Living God
subpoints: The reasonableness of God's existence: Psalm 14:1 — 'the fool has said in his heart there is no God'; creation is a masterpiece far too complex to be the product of a random explosion; to believe otherwise is irrational

The responsibility of God's unconditional love: John 3:16 — 'for God so loved the world that he gave'; love is an action word; if God were in our place, we would have destroyed humanity; instead he gave and kept giving

The reassurance of God's eternity: God is from everlasting to everlasting; he does not die; you cannot put your hope in something that dies; God told Moses 'I AM THAT I AM' — from eternity to eternity; nothing escapes him
His name is worthy of trust — as God swore to Abraham by himself (Genesis 22:16), because there was no one greater; we are in God's custody, in God's trust

outline.title: Why Our Hope Is Built on the Savior
subpoints: The promise of God in the garden: Genesis 3:14-15 — redemption was planned from the beginning; the seed of the woman who will bruise the serpent's head; God's promises are 'Yes and Amen' — unlike human promises, God always fulfills
The proclamation of the gospel: we are here for the good news; the gospel is the thing that keeps us toiling and striving; 'God is most glorified when we most evangelize'

The pains of Golgotha: Hebrews 2:9-10 — he was made lower than the angels, crowned with glory through suffering; without the cross there is no hope; Christ had to suffer; the righteousness of God had to be satisfied

The prophetless (empty) grave: the resurrection is the main pillar of Christianity; every religion attacks Christians on this point; the disciples went to their deaths because they had seen the risen Christ

outline.title: Living Out the Hope: Pilgrims, Not Settlers
subpoints: We are pilgrims passing through — heaven is our home; like Abraham who lived in tents all his life while Lot chose the city (which was destroyed), we are looking for the city whose builder and maker is God
Persecution is part of the game — 2 Timothy 3:12: 'all who live godly in Christ Jesus will suffer persecution'; do not try to avoid it; the reason there is hope today is because Christ suffered
God planted a church in Tyre — a stronghold of Hezbollah — because our hope is set on the living God; Tyre Church is growing, feeding people, educating people, giving jobs, healing people
Where is your hope set? Trust the Giver, not the gift — do not lose the Giver for the sake of what he gives; keep your eyes on Jesus at all times

summary:
Pastor Mohammad Yamout, pastor of Tire (Tyre) Church in Lebanon, brings the morning sermon as a guest speaker while visiting the congregation that supports Tini, one of their missionaries. He preaches from 1 Timothy 4:10 — 'for to this end we toil and strive because we have our hope set on the living God who is the Savior of all, especially those who believe' — in the context of widespread global hopelessness: COVID-19, civil wars across the Middle East, Lebanon's devastating economic collapse and the Beirut explosion, and political despair in America.

The sermon argues that hope in the living God is not wishful thinking but a firm foundation built on four pillars. The living God is reasonable — the universe's complexity demands a master mind and only a fool denies it (Psalm 14:1). He is responsible — his love is unconditional and active, demonstrated by giving his Son while we were enemies (John 3:16). He is eternal — from everlasting to everlasting, unlike every government and institution in which people misplace their hope. And he is the Savior — whose redemptive plan was set in motion in Genesis 3:15, fulfilled at Golgotha, confirmed by the empty tomb, and proclaimed in the gospel.

Pastor Yamout speaks from personal experience: prison, tuberculosis, civil war, and flying in thin metal tubes across the sky have all been occasions when his hope in the eternal, living God was his only anchor. He closes with a call to live as pilgrims rather than settlers, to expect persecution as part of the Christian life, and to remain focused on the gospel as the reason God leaves us here. He describes what that hope looks like in practice: Tire Church, planted in a Hezbollah stronghold, growing and serving its community because its hope is set on the living God.
transcript:
## Scripture Reading: 1 Timothy 4:6-11

*If you instruct the brethren in these things, you will be a good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished in the words of faith and of the good doctrine which you have carefully followed. But reject profane and old wives' fables, and exercise yourself toward godliness. For bodily exercise profits a little, but godliness is profitable for all things, having promise of the life that now is and of that which is to come. This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptance. For to this end we both labor and suffer reproach, because we trust in the living God, who is the Savior of all men, especially of those who believe. These things command and teach.*

## Introduction

Good morning. It is great to be here. I am very grateful that God has given me the opportunity to share his Word and his work in us and through us in Lebanon and the Middle East. We are very thankful for your support to Tini throughout the last two years. God is using Tini. She is a great lady and we love her and we thank you for supporting her in your prayers and financially.

This morning, the pastor mentioned that we are going to sing *My Hope Is Built on Nothing Less Than Jesus' Blood.* And I did not know that ahead of time. God has a way of arranging things. Sometimes we want everything to be organized and this done at this time and that done at that time, and we leave the Holy Spirit out. But the Holy Spirit has things to do. When you come to God's house, make no mistake — God has something for you. You are not here to socialize. You are here for God to speak to you. Never allow your church to turn into a social club.

We definitely live in hopeless times. Hopelessness is evident on all levels and everywhere. The world seems hopeless in front of COVID-19. Many people groups, especially in the Middle East, seem hopeless in front of civil wars raging in Syria, Yemen, Libya, Iraq, Afghanistan, Somalia, and lately in Nagorno-Karabakh in Armenia. In Lebanon, the Lebanese people are losing hope due to corrupt leaders, economic crisis, the Beirut explosion, and COVID-19. In America, people are losing hope because of elections and politics.

And this morning, I want to take you to the scripture from the passage we just read — verse 10: *For to this end we toil and strive because we have our hope set on the living God, who is the Savior of all people, especially of those who believe.*

For to this end we toil and strive. We work hard. We labor day in and day out. We serve God and we do not stop — because we should not lose hope. Our hope is built on Jesus Christ, on the living God. That is the difference between us and the world. And that difference should be lived out and seen by everybody.

## Hope: The Third Pillar

1 Corinthians 13:13: *So now faith, hope, and love abide.* If our hope is not built on a firm foundation, then our faith and love will be wavering — just like our hope. Hebrews 11:1: *Faith is the assurance of things hoped for.* If we are to have faith that reaches eternity, our hope has to be eternal.

Many times we base our hopes on things that do not last, on things that are not firm, on things that are not sure. *I hope so.* What do you mean? On what basis do you hope? Our hope should be built on the living God — the great I AM, who is the Alpha and the Omega. Our hope should be built on the Savior, Jesus Christ, and his redemptive plan that spreads from the beginning of time to the end of time.

## Why Our Hope Is Built on the Living God

**The reasonableness of God's existence.**

You know what is awesome? As believers this morning, we know that God is with us. He is present. We are here to speak to a person who is alive.

I wondered sometimes: what made the disciples go into all the world and spread the gospel? What made them die? What made them suffer? What drove them? When Jesus stood on the mountain and said *go into all the world and preach the gospel* — how did they do that? Here they were afraid. They were being persecuted. Their Lord had died. And then they went and changed the world. Why? Because on that mountain they were standing in front of a man they had seen crucified, buried in a grave — and alive again. That is what drove them. What all of us fear is death. Today — oh please, I might get COVID! And Jesus rose from the dead. He gave us victory over death.

Psalm 14:1: *The fool has said in his heart, "There is no God."* You would have to be a fool to believe otherwise. This universe — this art, this masterpiece — so intricate, so awesome — a human being who can dream, think, conquer, and create great things — came from a boom? A big bang? That does not compute. There is a brain. There is a mind. There is a Master Mind who created man and everything around man.

**The responsibility of God's unconditional love.**

You put your hope in somebody who loves you. Having loved his own, he loved them to the end — no reservations. If I were in God's place when man sinned in the garden, I would have deleted everything and started over. But God was responsible. He loved and he loved responsibly.

John 3:16: *For God so loved the world that he gave.* Love is an action word. You cannot say you love someone and stand idle. God gave. He did something. And when you have someone who is responsible and who loves you, you put your hope in him.

Genesis 22:16: God swore to Abraham by himself — *by myself I have sworn* — because there was no one greater. Imagine having a note signed by the wealthiest person in the world. You are in God's custody, in God's trust. The other word for hope is trust.

**The reassurance of God's eternity.**

God is from everlasting to everlasting. He does not die. Can you put your hope in someone who might die any time? Can you trust in a government? Any government? He is in office — God is in office. Make no mistake. He is in charge. He is on the throne. Whether Trump or Biden is in office — it does not matter. He will make the call. When men do not listen to him, he uses a donkey. He does what he wants.

To Moses he said: *I AM THAT I AM.* From eternity to eternity, I exist. Nothing escapes me. This is comforting to me. When I was in prison, this was comforting. When I had tuberculosis, comforting. When I was in the civil war, comforting. When I am on a plane in a thin metal can that flies — with no idea whether someone left a screw unscrewed — I say: God is holding this plane. If it is not of God, I am not going.

## Why Our Hope Is Built on the Savior

**The promise of God in the garden.**

Redemption was planned from the very beginning. Genesis 3:14-15: God said to the serpent, *I will put enmity between you and the woman, and between your offspring and her offspring. He shall bruise your head, and you shall bruise his heel.* From the beginning, God promised. God's promises are Yes and Amen — unlike human promises that cannot be fulfilled. From Adam to Abraham to Moses to David to Jesus, God fulfilled his promise. Even to the last minute, Jesus was saying: *Father, if you can pass this cup...* But he could not pass it. Because he had promised to save humanity. And that was the end of it.

**The proclamation of the gospel.**

We believe in a Savior because of the gospel — the good news. Your life should be centered around the good news. We are here for the good news. When I got saved, I was so excited about Jesus that I wanted to go home and meet him in person. And God said: no, Muhammad, I want you here. I have been trying to die for Jesus for forty years and God keeps saying not yet.

You have to be in a strange state of mind to desire to stay on this earth rather than being in the presence of your Lord and Master. Our desire should be to be with Jesus. The reason we stay here is for the gospel. God is most glorified when we most evangelize. That is what gives us hope. That is what keeps us toiling and striving — because the gospel we preach and live gives hope to this dying world.

**The pains of Golgotha.**

Christ my hope is built on appeasing the righteousness of God through his blood. Hebrews 2:9-10: *We see him who for a little while was made lower than the angels, namely Jesus, crowned with glory and honor because of the suffering of death, so that by the grace of God he might taste death for everyone. For it was fitting that he for whom and by whom all things exist, in bringing many sons to glory, should make the founder of their salvation perfect through suffering.*

It was suffering. There was no way out. He had to suffer so that we might have hope. Many people today compromise their stand to avoid persecution. My friends, persecution is part of the game for believers. 2 Timothy 3:12: *All who desire to live godly in Christ Jesus will suffer persecution.* You are in a fight. Get moving and fight. Do not try to avoid persecution — it is part of the game.

**The empty grave.**

The grave could not contain Jesus. He is risen. This is the main pillar Christianity is built on. Every other religion attacks us on this point. Do you know what drove the disciples to go out and spread the gospel — ready to be eaten by beasts, burned at the stake? They had seen the risen Lord. Jesus had been crucified and buried, and then he was standing on a mountain, alive. That is what drove them. Because they had hope — hope that Jesus is risen, and they will rise with him.

## Living Out the Hope

We are going to a better place. Earth is not my home. I am just passing by. I am a pilgrim. Many times God wants us to be pilgrims and we are trying to be settlers — building and hoarding and accumulating. What are you doing? You are just passing by.

I love the contrast of Abraham and Lot. Abraham was a rich man — but he was a pilgrim. He lived in a tent all his life. He never desired to have a city, except that city whose builder and maker is God. Lot chose the beautiful plain and settled in a city. What happened to the city? Every city you build on this earth is going down. Look to heaven. What God gives you of resources — use it for his glory.

You are the light of the world. The only reason God leaves you here is to shine for Jesus. Let your light shine before men that they may see your good works. We are a people of good works. Do not do good to appease your conscience — do good to live your life as a Christian, to be like your Master.

That is how we bring hope to this world. They come to Tyre. Lebanon today is devastated — the economy is flat, people are going hungry, no jobs. And in the middle of Tyre, Tire Church is growing, expanding, loving people, feeding people, educating people, caring for people, healing people, giving jobs. You know why? Because our hope is built on the living God, on the Savior of all.

Tyre is a stronghold of Hezbollah. There was no way a church could be planted there. God is stronger than Hezbollah, and he planted a church.

Where is your hope set today? Trust in the Giver, not the gift. Do not lose the Giver for the sake of what he gives. Keep your eyes on Jesus at all times.

*Father, we thank you, Lord, for your goodness. For your Word and its clarity. We pray that we will obey and not try to be smart. Help us to purely obey and set our hope on the living God and the Savior of all. We honor you, we glorify you this morning. We lift your name on high. Bless us with many blessings, O God, and help us and guide us. In Jesus' name, amen.*
